page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with 2 threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the x86_64 architecture processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a large 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results show strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.67 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.51 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory write operations show some limitations; the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated as weak at 4032.62, and the large-block memory write bandwidth is 22.85 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for deep learning. This is reflected in average performance for small LLM inference (135M model) at 119.64 tokens/sec for text generation, though performance drops to a poor 4.97 tokens/sec on medium 7B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4s_v7 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or massive multi-threaded parallelization. The dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed make it suitable for applications requiring strong single-threaded execution, such as lightweight web serving, basic Redis caching, and general-purpose utility workloads. It is less suited for heavy database operations or large-scale machine learning inference due to its weak multi-threaded scaling and database benchmark results.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 16.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, its x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks reveal strong single-threaded performance, highlighted by high gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds, alongside average static web serving and Redis performance. However, multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. This virtual machine is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced, single-thread-heavy workloads such as lightweight web serving, caching, and basic application hosting, while being less optimal for intensive database operations or large-scale multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and strong single-threaded performance, suitable for lightweight web serving and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency dedicated Intel processors, optimized for strong single-threaded workloads and standard application hosting.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression benchmarks
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No local storage or bundled GPUs, but Premium Storage capable
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and general-purpose utility tasks
